fixes_applied: tests/test_manifest.yaml: reconcile manifest with test.sh actual scoring. test.sh has 7 bash inner F2P gates (weights 0.108/0.108/0.060/0.072/0.084/0.084/0.084 = 0.60) plus 2 python-tail F2P gates at 0.20 each (sum 0.40, total 1.00) — but manifest declares only the 2 upstream gates at 0.5 each. Update manifest to enumerate all 9 F2P gates with weights matching test.sh effective scoring: trait_load_metrics_uses_authinfo (0.108), metric_impls_migrated_authinfo (0.108), filters_get_dimension_uses_authinfo (0.060), lib_get_auth_event_metrics_uses_authinfo (0.072), org_scope_auth_events_registered (0.084), profile_scope_auth_events_registered (0.084), three_auth_events_route_registrations (0.084), upstream_auth_metrics_migration (0.20), upstream_org_profile_endpoints (0.20). Sum = 1.00. P2P_REGRESSION p2p_upstream_key_files_exist retained as informational (weight 0).
f2p_added: trait_load_metrics_uses_authinfo (0.108): AuthEventMetric trait load_metrics signature migrated from MerchantId to AuthInfo
metric_impls_migrated_authinfo (0.108): >=4 metric impl files in auth_events/metrics/ migrated to AuthInfo + auth.set_filter_clause
lib_get_auth_event_metrics_uses_authinfo (0.072): analytics/src/lib.rs get_auth_event_metrics signature uses AuthInfo
profile_scope_auth_events_registered (0.084): /profile scope registers profile-level auth_events handler
three_auth_events_route_registrations (0.084): >=3 auth_events route registrations or >=3 distinct handler families (merchant+org+profile)
notes: Pre-flag was TEST_NARROW because manifest only listed 2 F2P @ 0.5 each on a 17-file refactor. Investigation: actual test.sh contains 9 F2P gates (7 bash inner + 2 python-tail upstream) summing to 1.00, providing fine-grained coverage of the refactor (trait sig, 12-file metric impl migration with threshold >=4, filters fn sig, lib provider sig, /org route, /profile route, multi-handler count, set_filter_clause spread >=6, both org+profile router handlers). Coverage is adequate; the issue was strictly manifest documentation. test.sh logic aligns with gold patch (gold patch migrates 12 metric files, adds get_org_auth_event_metrics + get_profile_auth_event_metrics + sankey variants in router). Caveats: (1) all 9 F2P gates are structural grep-based (per CLAUDE.md <=40% structural rule, this is high — but Rust web refactor without compilation is hard to validate behaviorally; cargo check would require ~6GB memory which exceeds task.toml's 4GB cap, see fix_summary.md OOM note); (2) fix_summary.md is stale — claims gates run cargo check but current test.sh does not. Despite structural-heavy testing, the gates are diverse, locality-specific (different files/functions), and resistant to single-edit gaming. Prior Sonnet 4.6 vs Haiku 4.5 gap of 0.95 indicates test discriminates well. Manifest reconciliation is the only required fix; behavioral upgrade (adding cargo check) is out of scope and likely infeasible under the 4GB memory cap.
